# UC-22.13.56 · Baseline Update Documentation — Config Baseline Version Changes

## Description

We audit when official configuration baseline versions change and require an authorizing record for each update.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc and sourcetypes cip010_baseline_version, cip010_baseline_version`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Emit version bump events from CMDB or GitOps. (2) Require `auth_record_id` from CAB approval. (3) Alert compliance on missing linkage. (4) Archive prior baselines offline. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cip010_baseline_version`, `sourcetype=cip010_baseline_version``.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` `sourcetype=cip010_baseline_version`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Emit version bump events from CMDB or GitOps. (2) Require `auth_record_id` from CAB approval. (3) Alert compliance on missing linkage. (4) Archive prior baselines offline.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype=cip010_baseline_version earliest=-365d
| sort 0 - _time
| streamstats current=f latest(baseline_version) as prev_version by platform
| where baseline_version!=prev_version
| eval has_auth=if(isnotnull(auth_record_id),1,0)
| where has_auth=0
| table _time, platform, baseline_version, prev_version, owner, auth_record_id
```

Understanding this SPL

**Baseline Update Documentation — Config Baseline Version Changes (CIP-010-4 R1 Part 1.3)** — We audit when official configuration baseline versions change and require an authorizing record for each update.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` `sourcetype=cip010_baseline_version`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: cip010_baseline_version. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype=cip010_baseline_version, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• `streamstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by platform** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where baseline_version!=prev_version` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **has_auth** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where has_auth=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: _time, auth_record_id, baseline_version, has_auth, owner, platform, prev_version.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP firewall log, Windows Security, or the asset register—whichever the search is built to mirror. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (version bumps), Table (missing auth), Single value (violations)

## SPL

```spl
index=grc sourcetype=cip010_baseline_version earliest=-365d
| sort 0 - _time
| streamstats current=f latest(baseline_version) as prev_version by platform
| where baseline_version!=prev_version
| eval has_auth=if(isnotnull(auth_record_id),1,0)
| where has_auth=0
| table _time, platform, baseline_version, prev_version, owner, auth_record_id
```

## Visualization

Timeline (version bumps), Table (missing auth), Single value (violations). Key fields: `auth_record_id`, `baseline_version`, `has_auth`, `owner`, `platform`, `prev_version`.
